title: Companion Trials
description: Turns Minecraft’s new mannequin into an experimental companion that follows you, picks up swords, and defends its player and social options.
body:
![Companion-Trials-Logo](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c7cd5203d6279458ad0375b79b5a111187c52a1c.png)

![separatorbar](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/e2e4c4bbb0deed3af2790d6a57952e2be85ce40a.png)

Add NPCs in Minecraft to create more alive and immersive encounters. Introduce behaviors and reactions: expanded animations, contextual decisions, and responses to world events.

![Showcase](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f716820085e1c11dbf3aa121770967381334adcb.webp)

![separatorbar2](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f4b8bde9deb97f7a5a7b1f714ecd04d24a422127.png)

**Features**

*Dynamic Behaviors*
* Daily Routines - NPCs follow simple schedules (work, leisure, sleep) and change their activities based on the time of day and weather.
* Contextual Reactions - NPCs react naturally to damage, fire, explosions, and environmental changes (water, lava, player proximity).

*Enhanced Movement & Pathfinding*
* Natural Movement - NPCs avoid obstacles with smoother movements and varied pacing instead of sudden turns.
* Block Interaction - NPCs seamlessly use doors, climb stairs, and stop in front of obvious dangers.

*Social Interactions*
* Conversations & Greetings - Experience small dialogues or gestures between NPCs and with the player.
* Grouping - NPCs can form groups or follow collective routines (such as gathering at markets or teamwork).

*Customization & API*
* In-Game Settings - Toggle features, control aggressiveness, behavior density, and detection ranges directly in-game.
* Modding API - Includes a limited API allowing other mods to add new custom behaviors or conditions.

**Dependencies:**
* Fabric Loader 1.21.10 (required)
* Fabric API (required)
* Mod Menu (optional, for quick in-game config access)
* Cloth Config (optional, for detailed config screens)

Server-Side & Client-Side - Requires installation on both the server (for the AI, routines, and pathfinding) and the client (for the configuration screens and animations).
